en: I bring with me, on the train from London, a message from Westminster.
No, no, no, no. Not a message. Not a message. An instruction, in fact.
You must say nothing. Say nothing about the present situation in this city.
Say nothing about hunger. Say nothing about jobs. Say nothing about pay.
But those in greener pastures, they may speak. They may raise their voices.
But here in the smoke, you men and women, Irish and Italian.
